name: search_loads
description: Search the load board for loads matching the carrier's lane and equipment preferences. All parameters are optional — if the carrier doesn't specify a preference, omit the field and you'll get the next N available loads. Call this after you know the carrier's origin/destination and/or equipment type.

origin.description: City or state the carrier is picking up from. Fuzzy matched — 'Dallas' or 'TX' both work.

destination.description: City or state the carrier wants to deliver to. Fuzzy matched.

equipment_type.description: Equipment the carrier has. Exact match against one of the values below. Ask the carrier which they drive if unclear.

pickup_date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) if the carrier is asking for a specific day. Usually omitted.

max_results.description: How many loads to return. Default 3 — aim for 1 great match, not a menu.

load_id.description: Pass this exactly to evaluate_offer and log_call

loadboard_rate.description: Listed rate in USD. This is the anchor for negotiation.

pitching_notes: If count=0, apologize and end the call with outcome=no_match.
Pitch with origin -> destination, pickup day/time, equipment, miles, and the rate as a round USD figure ('twenty-four hundred' not '2400.00').
Only mention the notes field if there's something actionable (e.g., 'drop-and-hook at destination', 'tarps required').
